title: 2023年度 第1回 英検2級 リーディング

vocabulary.example: You have to use your own judgment to decide.
His income is not enough to support his family.
Breaking social conventions can be risky.
His explanation was a bit vague.
Smoking is harmful to your health.
He decided to confront the problem.
We need to alter our plans.
The parade proceeded slowly to the center of town.
The teacher illustrated the concept with examples.
People used carriages pulled by horses.
She lived in a world of fantasy.
A new car is a luxury we cannot afford.
She asked the clerk to wrap them individually.
The contract is legally binding.
They are financially independent.
Professor Smith stressed how important it was.
Firefighters sprayed water onto the fire.
Each player tries to build an empire.
There was a sense of urgency in her voice.
The hottest part of the flame was blue.
They were very disappointed in her.
I set aside some food for you.
The electricity had to be shut off.
They will be bringing out a new smartphone.
The Amazon River flows into the Atlantic Ocean.
Applicants must apply in person.
Jason is in charge of checking the floor.
You can go, provided you finish your homework.
The animal protects its offspring.
She bought it on impulse.
It was a great triumph for the team.
Freedom of religion is a basic right.
She reminded me of my appointment.
They defended their territory bravely.
We honored the veterans at the ceremony.
The activity engaged all the students.
The dog buried the bone in the garden.
The workers demanded higher pay.
She was awarded the Nobel Prize.
We cannot rule out the possibility.
We need to cut down on expenses.
She hung up the phone quickly.
She takes after her mother.
Don't fall for that trick.
Stop picking on your little brother.
The flowers give off a sweet scent.
Many countries are moving toward a cashless society.
The two newspapers were rivals.
Psychologists study the purpose of dreams.
The participants were woken up several times.
She carried the laundry to the river.
Machines give people more time for leisure.
Many countries are moving away from traditional currency.
The coin comes in several denominations.
Cash transactions are becoming less common.
New security features prevent counterfeit coins.
Coins are still needed for vending machines.
Shakespeare is the most famous English playwright.
This is one of Shakespeare's greatest comedies.
The original manuscript was discovered in a library.
Enrollment for the new course begins next week.
The tuition includes all course materials.
The machine was designed to produce textiles more quickly.
The Industrial Revolution changed manufacturing forever.
He obtained a patent for his new invention.
Many young people have aspirations of becoming entrepreneurs.
Her determination helped her overcome many obstacles.

sections.name: 大問1
sections.instruction: 次の(1)から(20)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを1，2，3，4の中から一つ選びなさい。

questions.text:
A : Dave asked me to marry him. Do you think I should say yes?
B : You have to use your own ( 1 ) to decide. No one else can do it for you.
questions.translation:
A：デイブがプロポーズしてきたの。受けるべきだと思う？
B：自分の( 1 )で決めなきゃ。他の誰にもできないよ。
choiceAnalysis: income=収入→決断の文脈に不適切
convention=慣習→不適切
judgment=判断→正解。💡 use your own judgment=自分で判断する。💡 2級超頻出の表現
geography=地理→不適切

questions.text:
A : What did you think of my essay, Jill?
B : Well, some of the explanations you gave are a bit ( 2 ). Maybe you should make those parts clearer.
questions.translation:
A：私のエッセイどう思った、ジル？
B：うーん、説明のいくつかがちょっと( 2 )かな。もう少し明確にした方がいいかも。
choiceAnalysis: harmful=有害な→エッセイの文脈に不適切
previous=以前の→不適切
certain=確かな→「不明確にする」と矛盾
vague=曖昧な→正解。💡 vague=はっきりしない。make clearer(明確にする)がヒント

questions.text: Colin had been getting bad grades in math for a long time, but he did not do anything about it. Finally, he decided to ( 3 ) the problem and ask his teacher for help.
questions.translation: コリンは長い間数学の成績が悪かったが何もしなかった。ついに、問題に( 3 )して先生に助けを求めることにした。
choiceAnalysis: alter=変える→問題を変えるは不自然
impress=感動させる→不適切
honor=称える→不適切
confront=立ち向かう→正解。💡 confront the problem=問題に立ち向かう。Finally(ついに)がヒント

questions.text: The St. Patrick's Day parade started on 10th Avenue and ( 4 ) slowly to the center of town.
questions.translation: セント・パトリックス・デーのパレードは10番街から始まり、町の中心へゆっくり( 4 )した。
choiceAnalysis: illustrated=描写した→パレードの動きに不適切
reminded=思い出させた→不適切
proceeded=進んだ→正解。💡 proceed slowly=ゆっくり進む。パレードの動きを表す
defended=守った→不適切

questions.text: Before there were motor vehicles, people often used ( 5 ) that were pulled by horses to make long journeys.
questions.translation: 自動車ができる前、人々は長旅のために馬に引かれる( 5 )をよく使った。
choiceAnalysis: carriages=馬車→正解。💡 carriages pulled by horses=馬に引かれる馬車。💡 motor vehiclesとの対比がヒント
fantasies=空想→乗り物ではない
puzzles=パズル→不適切
luxuries=贅沢品→乗り物の文脈に不適切

questions.text: Jane bought five silk handkerchiefs. Because she was going to give each one to a different friend, she asked the salesclerk to wrap them ( 6 ).
questions.translation: ジェーンは絹のハンカチを5枚買った。それぞれ別の友人に渡すので、店員に( 6 )包むよう頼んだ。
choiceAnalysis: legally=合法的に→包装に不適切
financially=経済的に→不適切
accidentally=偶然に→不適切
individually=個別に→正解。💡 each one to a different friend→individually(個別に)包装。因果関係に注目

questions.text: In the first lecture, Professor Smith ( 7 ) how important it was to take good notes. He mentioned it three or four times.
questions.translation: 最初の講義で、スミス教授は良いノートを取ることの重要性を( 7 )した。彼は3、4回それに言及した。
choiceAnalysis: engaged=従事させた→重要性の強調に不適切
divided=分けた→不適切
buried=埋めた→不適切
stressed=強調した→正解。💡 stressed how important=重要性を強調。mentioned 3-4 times(繰り返し言及)がヒント

questions.text: Yesterday, there was a small fire at a house on the street where Ben lives. Firefighters ( 8 ) water onto the fire and soon put it out.
questions.translation: 昨日、ベンの住む通りの家で小さな火災があった。消防士が火に水を( 8 )し、すぐに消火した。
choiceAnalysis: sprayed=噴霧した→正解。💡 spray water=水を噴霧する・放水する。消火活動の定型表現
demanded=要求した→消火の文脈に不適切
awarded=授与した→不適切
punished=罰した→不適切

questions.text: In the game Invasion!, each player tries to take over other countries and build an ( 9 ).
questions.translation: ゲーム「インベージョン！」では、各プレイヤーが他国を征服して( 9 )を築こうとする。
choiceAnalysis: urgency=緊急性→ゲームの目標ではない
offspring=子孫→不適切
empire=帝国→正解。💡 take over countries + build an empire=国を征服して帝国を築く
impulse=衝動→不適切

questions.text: In science class, Ms. Dixon lit a candle and asked her students to look at the ( 10 ). She told them that the hottest part was where it was blue.
questions.translation: 理科の授業で、ディクソン先生がろうそくに火をつけ、生徒に( 10 )を見るよう言った。最も熱い部分は青い所だと教えた。
choiceAnalysis: triumph=勝利→ろうそくに不適切
religion=宗教→不適切
flame=炎→正解。💡 candle + blue + hottest part=ろうそくの炎の説明
luggage=荷物→不適切

questions.text: Margaret's parents finally bought her a piano after she promised to practice every day, but she quit playing after just two months. They were very ( 11 ) her.
questions.translation: マーガレットは毎日練習すると約束してピアノを買ってもらったが、2ヶ月でやめた。両親はとても彼女に( 11 )した。
choiceAnalysis: capable of=～できる→がっかりの文脈に不適切
inspired by=～に触発される→不適切
attracted to=～に惹かれる→不適切
disappointed in=～にがっかりする→正解。💡 約束を守らなかった→disappointed in。前置詞inに注意

questions.text:
A : I'm sorry that I came home late. Did you have dinner without me?
B : Yes. We were very hungry, but I ( 12 ) some food for you. I'll heat it up now.
questions.translation:
A：遅く帰ってごめん。私なしで夕食食べた？
B：うん。すごくお腹減ってたけど、あなたのために食事を( 12 )おいたわ。今温めるね。
choiceAnalysis: cut down=削減する→食事の文脈に不適切
hung up=電話を切る→不適切
took after=似ている→不適切
set aside=取っておく→正解。💡 set aside food=食事を取っておく。💡 heat it upが取っておいた証拠

questions.text: Due to construction work on Bayside Street, the electricity to the houses on the street had to be ( 13 ) for two hours.
questions.translation: ベイサイドストリートの工事のため、通りの家の電気を2時間( 13 )しなければならなかった。
choiceAnalysis: heard of=聞いたことがある→電気に不適切
turned over=ひっくり返す→不適切
shut off=止める→正解。💡 shut off electricity=電気を止める・停電にする。工事→停電の因果
ruled out=除外する→不適切

questions.text: Tau Electronics has confirmed that it will be ( 14 ) its new smartphone next month. The release has been delayed for several months due to technical issues.
questions.translation: タウ・エレクトロニクスは来月新しいスマートフォンを( 14 )することを確認した。技術的問題で数ヶ月遅れていた。
choiceAnalysis: bringing out=発売する→正解。💡 bring out=発売する・世に出す。release(発売)と同義
falling for=騙される→不適切
picking on=いじめる→不適切
giving off=発散する→不適切

questions.text: Water that falls as snow and rain in the Andes Mountains travels thousands of kilometers down the Amazon River and eventually ( 15 ) the Atlantic Ocean.
questions.translation: アンデス山脈に雪や雨として降った水は、アマゾン川を何千キロも流れ下り、最終的に大西洋に( 15 )。
choiceAnalysis: flows into=流れ込む→正解。💡 flow into the ocean=海に流れ込む。川→海の自然な表現
runs across=横切る→川が海を横切るのは不自然
hands over=引き渡す→不適切
digs up=掘り出す→不適切

questions.text: Applicants for jobs at Swandon Foods must apply ( 16 ). They are not allowed to mail their application forms. Instead, they must take them to the store manager or his assistant.
questions.translation: スワンドン・フーズの求職者は( 16 )で申し込まなければならない。郵送は不可。代わりに店長か副店長に持参すること。
choiceAnalysis: at ease=気楽に→申し込み方法ではない
at length=長々と→不適切
in person=直接→正解。💡 not mail + take them to=郵送不可→直接持参=in person
in detail=詳細に→不適切

questions.text: The students in Jason's class take turns to look after things in the classroom. This week, Jason is ( 17 ) of checking that there is no trash on the floor at the end of each day.
questions.translation: ジェイソンのクラスでは交代で教室の世話をする。今週ジェイソンは毎日の終わりに床にゴミがないか確認する( 17 )だ。
choiceAnalysis: for fear=恐れて→担当の文脈に不適切
in charge=担当して→正解。💡 in charge of=～の担当。take turns(交代)がヒント
on behalf=代わりに→不適切
by way=途中で→不適切

questions.text: Jenny is an excellent cook. Her soups and stews are as good as ( 18 ) that are served in a restaurant.
questions.translation: ジェニーは料理上手だ。彼女のスープやシチューはレストランで出される( 18 )と同じくらいおいしい。
choiceAnalysis: none→as good as noneは「良くない」の意味
any→正解。💡 as good as any=どれにも劣らない。💡 比較級のanyは「どんな～でも」
other→文法的に不自然
few→as good as fewは不自然

questions.text:
A : Mom, can I go to the park to play with Jimmy later?
B : Yes, ( 19 ) you finish your homework first.
questions.translation:
A：ママ、後でジミーと公園に行ってもいい？
B：いいわよ、まず宿題を終わらせ( 19 )ば。
choiceAnalysis: provide→動詞原形は文法的に不正確
provided=～という条件で→正解。💡 provided (that)=～という条件で。if/as long asと同義
to provide→不定詞は不自然
only provide→文法的に不正確

questions.text: Ms. Misawa is very rich and lives in a huge house. Her kitchen is four times ( 20 ) other people's kitchens.
questions.translation: 三沢さんはとても裕福で大きな家に住んでいる。彼女のキッチンは他の人のキッチンの4倍の( 20 )だ。
choiceAnalysis: large from→文法的に不正確
size from→文法的に不正確
the large of→形容詞は不可
the size of→正解。💡 X times the size of Y=YのX倍の大きさ。💡 倍数表現の定型パターン

sections.name: 大問2
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，Bを読み，その文意にそって(21)から(26)までの(　)に入れるのに最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: Any Change?
paragraphs: Long ago, humans did not use money. Because they often could not produce everything that they needed, they traded some of their goods for goods made by others. Gradually, the goods that they exchanged were replaced by cash. For hundreds of years, metal coins and paper bills that can be exchanged for goods and services have been produced. Cash is convenient for many people because it is easy to carry. At the same time, though, it ( 21 ). Another disadvantage is that criminals have been able to produce fake coins and bills.

In the middle of the 20th century, plastic credit cards were introduced. They had security features to prevent them from being used by anyone except their owners. At first, their use was limited to wealthy people. Over time, however, they became ( 22 ). In the last few years, apps for smartphones that can be used in the same way as credit cards have also become popular. Because of this, some people are suggesting that we may soon see the end of cash.

Supporters of a "cashless" society in which all payments are made electronically argue that it would have several benefits. For example, people would not have to worry about keeping their wallets safe. However, some people are concerned that they might be unable to pay for the things they need because of a software error or a broken smartphone. Moreover, some people do not have bank accounts or credit cards, so their only option is to use coins and bills. ( 23 ), it seems as though societies will continue to use cash.
translations: 昔、人間はお金を使わなかった。必要なもの全てを生産できないことが多かったため、自分の品物の一部を他者の品物と交換した。次第に交換していた品物は現金に取って代わられた。何百年もの間、商品やサービスと交換できる硬貨や紙幣が生産されてきた。現金は持ち運びやすいので多くの人にとって便利だ。しかし同時に、( 21 )。もう一つの欠点は、犯罪者が偽の硬貨や紙幣を作ることができたことだ。
20世紀半ばにプラスチック製のクレジットカードが導入された。所有者以外の使用を防ぐセキュリティ機能があった。当初は裕福な人に限定されていた。しかし時が経つにつれ、( 22 )なった。ここ数年でクレジットカードと同じように使えるスマートフォンアプリも普及した。このため、まもなく現金の終わりが来ると示唆する人もいる。
全ての支払いが電子的に行われる「キャッシュレス」社会の支持者は、いくつかのメリットがあると主張する。例えば、財布を安全に保つ心配がなくなる。しかし、ソフトウェアエラーやスマートフォンの故障で必要なものの支払いができなくなる懸念もある。さらに銀行口座やクレジットカードを持たない人もおり、硬貨や紙幣を使うしか選択肢がない。( 23 )、社会は現金を使い続けるように思われる。
sentencePairs: Long ago, humans did not use money.
昔、人間はお金を使わなかった。
Because they often could not produce everything that they needed, they traded some of their goods for goods made by others.
必要なもの全てを生産できないことが多かったため、自分の品物の一部を他者の品物と交換した。
Gradually, the goods that they exchanged were replaced by cash.
次第に交換していた品物は現金に取って代わられた。
Cash is convenient for many people because it is easy to carry.
現金は持ち運びやすいので多くの人にとって便利だ。
At the same time, though, it ( 21 ).
しかし同時に、( 21 )。
Another disadvantage is that criminals have been able to produce fake coins and bills.
もう一つの欠点は、犯罪者が偽の硬貨や紙幣を作ることができたことだ。
In the middle of the 20th century, plastic credit cards were introduced.
20世紀半ばにプラスチック製のクレジットカードが導入された。
They had security features to prevent them from being used by anyone except their owners.
所有者以外の使用を防ぐセキュリティ機能があった。
At first, their use was limited to wealthy people.
当初は裕福な人に限定されていた。
Over time, however, they became ( 22 ).
しかし時が経つにつれ、( 22 )なった。
In the last few years, apps for smartphones that can be used in the same way as credit cards have also become popular.
ここ数年でクレジットカードと同じように使えるスマートフォンアプリも普及した。
Because of this, some people are suggesting that we may soon see the end of cash.
このため、まもなく現金の終わりが来ると示唆する人もいる。
However, some people are concerned that they might be unable to pay for the things they need because of a software error or a broken smartphone.
しかし、ソフトウェアエラーやスマートフォンの故障で必要なものの支払いができなくなる懸念もある。
Moreover, some people do not have bank accounts or credit cards, so their only option is to use coins and bills.
さらに銀行口座やクレジットカードを持たない人もおり、硬貨や紙幣を使うしか選択肢がない。
( 23 ), it seems as though societies will continue to use cash.
( 23 )、社会は現金を使い続けるように思われる。

choiceAnalysis: 紛失・盗難の可能性がある→正解。💡 disadvantage(欠点)の文脈。Another disadvantageに続く1つ目の欠点
オンラインショッピングに使われる→欠点ではない
リサイクルできる→欠点ではない
ほぼ全員に理解される→欠点ではない
より薄く軽くなった→カードの普及の文脈に不適切
よりカラフルに→不適切
より使いにくく→逆の文脈
より広く利用可能に→正解。💡 limited to wealthy→more widely available。限定→普及の変化
今のところ→正解。💡 For now=今のところ。懸念点→当面は現金が続くの結論
それまでは→前に具体的な時点がない
運が良ければ→客観的な結論に不適切
対照的に→対比ではなく結論

passages.label: B
passages.title: The Tale of Mejk Swenekafew
paragraphs: Recently, many people have been talking about "fake news" — news reports that are untrue. However, such reports have been around for a long time. They are sometimes used in order to get more people to read newspapers, watch TV programs, or visit online news sites. People also use fake news to spread their political or religious beliefs. However, ( 24 ) publishing fake news. In 1903 in the city of Clarksburg, West Virginia, fake news was used to check if a newspaper was really writing its own articles.

In the city, there were two rival newspapers, the Clarksburg Daily Telegram and the Clarksburg Daily News. The Daily Telegram's staff believed that the Daily News's reporters were ( 25 ). The Daily Telegram decided to check whether this was happening. It published a fake news story about a man who had been shot after an argument about a dog. The man's name was Mejk Swenekafew. Soon afterward, exactly the same news appeared in the Daily News. However, the reporters at the Daily News had not noticed that the name "Swenekafew" was actually "we fake news" written backward. They were forced to admit that they had copied the Daily Telegram's article.

These days, there is more pressure than ever on newspapers, news programs, and news websites to get more readers, viewers, and visitors. In order to do so, they need to report big news stories as quickly as possible. ( 26 ), they are constantly watching each other to make sure they have the latest stories. However, they need to be careful not to do the same thing that the Clarksburg Daily News did.
translations: 最近、「フェイクニュース」つまり事実でないニュース報道について多くの人が話題にしている。しかしそのような報道は古くから存在してきた。より多くの人に新聞を読ませたり、テレビを見させたり、ニュースサイトを訪問させるために使われることがある。政治的・宗教的信条を広めるためにも使われる。しかし、フェイクニュースを発信する理由はそれだけではない。1903年、ウェストバージニア州クラークスバーグでは、新聞が本当に自社で記事を書いているかを確認するためにフェイクニュースが使われた。

この街には2つのライバル紙、クラークスバーグ・デイリー・テレグラムとクラークスバーグ・デイリー・ニュースがあった。テレグラム紙のスタッフは、ニュース紙の記者が( 25 )と信じていた。テレグラム紙はこれが本当かどうか確認することにした。犬をめぐる口論の後に撃たれた男の偽ニュースを掲載した。その男の名前はメイク・スウェネカフューだった。間もなく、全く同じニュースがニュース紙に掲載された。しかしニュース紙の記者は「Swenekafew」が実は「we fake news」を逆から綴ったものであることに気づかなかった。テレグラム紙の記事をコピーしたことを認めざるを得なかった。
今日、新聞、ニュース番組、ニュースサイトには、より多くの読者、視聴者、訪問者を獲得するかつてない圧力がかかっている。そのためには大きなニュースをできるだけ早く報道する必要がある。( 26 )、彼らは常に最新の記事があるか互いを監視している。しかしクラークスバーグ・デイリー・ニュースと同じことをしないよう注意が必要だ。
sentencePairs: Recently, many people have been talking about "fake news" — news reports that are untrue.
最近、「フェイクニュース」つまり事実でないニュース報道について多くの人が話題にしている。
However, such reports have been around for a long time.
しかしそのような報道は古くから存在してきた。
They are sometimes used in order to get more people to read newspapers, watch TV programs, or visit online news sites.
より多くの人に新聞を読ませたり、テレビを見させたり、ニュースサイトを訪問させるために使われることがある。
People also use fake news to spread their political or religious beliefs.
政治的・宗教的信条を広めるためにも使われる。
However, ( 24 ) publishing fake news.
しかし、( 24 )フェイクニュースを発信する。
In 1903 in the city of Clarksburg, West Virginia, fake news was used to check if a newspaper was really writing its own articles.
1903年、ウェストバージニア州クラークスバーグでは、新聞が本当に自社で記事を書いているかを確認するためにフェイクニュースが使われた。
The Daily Telegram's staff believed that the Daily News's reporters were ( 25 ).
テレグラム紙のスタッフは、ニュース紙の記者が( 25 )と信じていた。
The Daily Telegram decided to check whether this was happening.
テレグラム紙はこれが本当かどうか確認することにした。
It published a fake news story about a man who had been shot after an argument about a dog.
犬をめぐる口論の後に撃たれた男の偽ニュースを掲載した。
The man's name was Mejk Swenekafew.
その男の名前はメイク・スウェネカフューだった。
Soon afterward, exactly the same news appeared in the Daily News.
間もなく、全く同じニュースがニュース紙に掲載された。
However, the reporters at the Daily News had not noticed that the name "Swenekafew" was actually "we fake news" written backward.
「Swenekafew」が実は「we fake news」を逆から綴ったものであることに気づかなかった。
They were forced to admit that they had copied the Daily Telegram's article.
テレグラム紙の記事をコピーしたことを認めざるを得なかった。
These days, there is more pressure than ever on newspapers, news programs, and news websites to get more readers, viewers, and visitors.
今日、新聞、ニュース番組、ニュースサイトには、より多くの読者、視聴者、訪問者を獲得するかつてない圧力がかかっている。
In order to do so, they need to report big news stories as quickly as possible.
そのためには大きなニュースをできるだけ早く報道する必要がある。
( 26 ), they are constantly watching each other to make sure they have the latest stories.
( 26 )、彼らは常に最新の記事があるか互いを監視している。
However, they need to be careful not to do the same thing that the Clarksburg Daily News did.
しかしクラークスバーグ・デイリー・ニュースと同じことをしないよう注意が必要だ。

choices: these are not the only reasons for
there are rules to stop people from
choiceAnalysis: これらだけがフェイクニュース発信の理由ではない→正解。💡 前文の理由(読者獲得、信条拡散)に加え「別の理由もある」→1903年の例に接続
人々のフェイクニュース発信を止めるルールがある→1903年の例と繋がらない
多くのサイトがフェイクニュースを発信していた→文脈に不適切
テレビ会社がフェイクニュース発信から始めた→不適切
イベントに参加していた→記事コピーの文脈に無関係
辞める計画をしていた→不適切
より多く給料をもらっていた→不適切
記事を盗んでいた→正解。💡 後文のcopy the article=記事をコピー→steal stories(記事を盗む)
それにもかかわらず→因果関係がある場面に不適切
偶然に→不適切
その結果→正解。💡 早く報道する必要→As a result(その結果)互いを監視。因果関係
一方で→対比ではない

sections.name: 大問3
sections.instruction: 次の英文A，B，Cの内容に関して，(27)から(38)までの質問に対して最も適切なものを選びなさい。

passages.label: A
passages.title: Speak-EZ Language School
paragraphs: Thank you very much for inquiring about Spanish lessons at Speak-EZ Language School. Speak-EZ Language School has been giving high-quality lessons for over 30 years, and our teachers have helped thousands of students achieve their goals. Whether you want to learn Spanish for business situations, to chat with friends, to pass exams, or just for the fun of learning, we can offer a suitable program for you.

You mentioned that because you studied Spanish in high school but had not used it for several years, you were not sure which lesson to take. Don't worry — we offer a free language skills test. You can take the test once you have decided to join Speak-EZ Language School. One of our instructors will use the results to help you pick the right program for your ability and your goals. You can also take a free 20-minute private lesson to get an idea of the methods that we use at Speak-EZ Language School.

We offer both private and group lessons. Group lessons can be a great way to make new friends. However, no more than eight people can join each group lesson, so spaces are limited. You can also try our Speak-EZ Chat Sessions. These are hosted by one of our instructors and give students of all levels a chance to chat freely. Speak-EZ Chat Sessions are available every weekday evening.
translations: Speak-EZランゲージスクールのスペイン語レッスンについてお問い合わせいただきありがとうございます。Speak-EZランゲージスクールは30年以上高品質なレッスンを提供しており、教師は何千人もの生徒の目標達成を支援してきました。ビジネス、友人との会話、試験対策、あるいは純粋に学ぶ楽しみのためなど、あなたに合ったプログラムを提供できます。
高校でスペイン語を勉強したが数年間使っていなかったため、どのレッスンを取るべきかわからないとのことですね。ご心配なく。無料の語学力テストがあります。Speak-EZランゲージスクールへの入学を決めたらテストを受けられます。インストラクターがその結果を使い、あなたの能力と目標に合ったプログラムを選ぶお手伝いをします。また、20分間の無料プライベートレッスンで当校の指導法を体験できます。
プライベートレッスンとグループレッスンの両方を提供しています。グループレッスンは新しい友人を作る素晴らしい方法です。ただし、各グループレッスンは8人までなので、席は限られています。Speak-EZチャットセッションもお試しいただけます。インストラクターが主催し、すべてのレベルの生徒が自由に会話できる機会を提供します。チャットセッションは毎週平日の夜に利用可能です。
sentencePairs: Thank you very much for inquiring about Spanish lessons at Speak-EZ Language School.
Speak-EZランゲージスクールのスペイン語レッスンについてお問い合わせいただきありがとうございます。
Speak-EZ Language School has been giving high-quality lessons for over 30 years.
Speak-EZランゲージスクールは30年以上高品質なレッスンを提供しています。
Whether you want to learn Spanish for business situations, to chat with friends, to pass exams, or just for the fun of learning, we can offer a suitable program for you.
ビジネス、友人との会話、試験対策、あるいは純粋に学ぶ楽しみのためなど、あなたに合ったプログラムを提供できます。
You mentioned that because you studied Spanish in high school but had not used it for several years, you were not sure which lesson to take.
高校でスペイン語を勉強したが数年間使っていなかったため、どのレッスンを取るべきかわからないとのことですね。
Don't worry — we offer a free language skills test.
ご心配なく。無料の語学力テストがあります。
One of our instructors will use the results to help you pick the right program for your ability and your goals.
インストラクターがその結果を使い、あなたの能力と目標に合ったプログラムを選ぶお手伝いをします。
We offer both private and group lessons.
プライベートレッスンとグループレッスンの両方を提供しています。
Group lessons can be a great way to make new friends.
グループレッスンは新しい友人を作る素晴らしい方法です。
However, no more than eight people can join each group lesson, so spaces are limited.
ただし、各グループレッスンは8人までなので、席は限られています。
Speak-EZ Chat Sessions are available every weekday evening.
チャットセッションは毎週平日の夜に利用可能です。

questions.question: What is one thing that Karen Taylor says about Speak-EZ Language School?
choices: It gives lessons in over 30 different languages.
It is planning to hire a new Spanish teacher.
It has teachers from all over the world.
It offers various types of courses.
choiceAnalysis: 30以上の言語→30年以上の歴史の読み違い
新しいスペイン語教師を雇う予定→本文にない
世界中の教師がいる→本文にない
様々なタイプのコースを提供→正解。💡 business, chat, exams, fun=various types of courses

questions.question: Ms. Mitchell said that she
choices: did not know which class to sign up for.
wanted more information about teaching methods.
was at high school with one of the instructors.
choiceAnalysis: どのクラスに申し込むかわからなかった→正解。💡 not sure which lesson to take=どのレッスンを取るかわからない
スペイン語を一度も勉強したことがない→高校で勉強した
指導法についての情報が欲しかった→本文にない
インストラクターと同じ高校→本文にない

questions.question: Speak-EZ Chat Sessions
choices: take place on Monday through Friday every week.
have a limited number of spaces per session.
are only available to advanced students.
focus on using foreign languages to make friends.
choiceAnalysis: 毎週月曜〜金曜に実施→正解。💡 every weekday evening=平日毎晩=Monday through Friday
席数が限られている→それはgroup lessonsの説明
上級者のみ→all levelsの生徒
外国語で友達を作ることに焦点→本文にない

passages.label: B
passages.title: An Extraordinary Machine
paragraphs: Most of the machines that people in developed nations use were invented during the last 200 years. They make tasks easier for people and give them more time for other tasks and for leisure. However, which of these machines has changed society the most? Even though people spend more time with their TVs, computers, and smartphones, some historians argue that the impact of these inventions has been small compared with that of washing machines.

Before washing machines, clothes and sheets were washed by hand. For most of history, this has involved carrying the laundry to a river or a lake, wetting it, and rubbing it with rocks, sand, or soap to remove the dirt. Then, the laundry had to be put in water again, and the extra water was usually removed to make drying easier. Even if people had water in their homes, the laundry would have to be rubbed against a special board or hit with pieces of wood to make it clean. It was hard work that took a long time.

The first washing machines were operated by hand, and they still required a lot of hard work. Discovering how to use electricity to power these machines was a challenge because the combination of water and electricity is very dangerous. However, during the first half of the 20th century, inventors created electric machines that were able to automatically do most of the steps involved in washing. Before long, these machines became common in homes in wealthier parts of the world.

Automatic washing machines gave people more time and energy for other activities than any other new technology did. They used some of this extra time and energy to study and teach their children. This, in turn, led to improvements in the quality of everyone's lives in the places where washing machines became common. Even today, many people in the world still wash their clothes by hand. This means that, over the next few decades, washing machines will probably continue to make a big difference to the lives of billions of humans.
translations: 先進国の人々が使う機械のほとんどは過去200年以内に発明された。これらは人々の作業を楽にし、他の作業や余暇のための時間を増やす。しかし、これらの機械のうちどれが社会を最も変えたのか？テレビやコンピュータ、スマートフォンに多くの時間を費やしているにもかかわらず、一部の歴史家はこれらの発明の影響は洗濯機に比べると小さいと主張する。
洗濯機ができる前、衣類やシーツは手で洗われていた。歴史の大半において、洗濯物を川や湖まで運び、濡らし、岩や砂や石けんでこすって汚れを落とすことが必要だった。その後再び水に浸し、乾きやすくするために余分な水を取り除く必要があった。家に水がある場合でも、洗濯板にこすりつけたり、木の棒で叩いたりしなければならなかった。長時間かかる重労働だった。
最初の洗濯機は手動式で、まだ多くの重労働が必要だった。水と電気の組み合わせは非常に危険なため、電気でこれらの機械を動かす方法の発見は課題だった。しかし20世紀前半、発明者たちは洗濯に関わるほとんどの工程を自動で行える電気式機械を発明した。まもなく、これらの機械は世界のより裕福な地域の家庭で一般的になった。
自動洗濯機は他のどの新技術よりも人々に多くの時間とエネルギーを与えた。彼らはその余分な時間とエネルギーの一部を勉強や子供の教育に使った。これが結果的に、洗濯機が普及した場所での全員の生活の質の向上につながった。今日でも世界の多くの人がまだ手で服を洗っている。今後数十年で洗濯機は何十億もの人間の生活に大きな変化をもたらし続けるだろう。
sentencePairs: Most of the machines that people in developed nations use were invented during the last 200 years.
先進国の人々が使う機械のほとんどは過去200年以内に発明された。
They make tasks easier for people and give them more time for other tasks and for leisure.
これらは人々の作業を楽にし、他の作業や余暇のための時間を増やす。
However, which of these machines has changed society the most?
しかし、これらの機械のうちどれが社会を最も変えたのか？
Some historians argue that the impact of these inventions has been small compared with that of washing machines.
一部の歴史家はこれらの発明の影響は洗濯機に比べると小さいと主張する。
Before washing machines, clothes and sheets were washed by hand.
洗濯機ができる前、衣類やシーツは手で洗われていた。
It was hard work that took a long time.
長時間かかる重労働だった。
The combination of water and electricity is very dangerous.
水と電気の組み合わせは非常に危険だ。
During the first half of the 20th century, inventors created electric machines that were able to automatically do most of the steps involved in washing.
20世紀前半、発明者たちは洗濯に関わるほとんどの工程を自動で行える電気式機械を発明した。
Automatic washing machines gave people more time and energy for other activities than any other new technology did.
自動洗濯機は他のどの新技術よりも人々に多くの時間とエネルギーを与えた。
They used some of this extra time and energy to study and teach their children.
彼らはその余分な時間とエネルギーの一部を勉強や子供の教育に使った。

questions.question: What do some historians say about the invention of washing machines?
choices: It happened due to an important change in society.
It led to the development of TVs, computers, and smartphones.
It has had a major impact on the natural environment.
It has had a greater effect on society than other modern inventions.
choiceAnalysis: 社会の重要な変化が原因→本文にない
テレビ等の開発につながった→本文にない
自然環境に大きな影響→本文にない
他の現代の発明より社会に大きな影響→正解。💡 impact has been small compared with that of washing machines=他の発明より洗濯機の影響が大きい

questions.question: Cleaning clothes and sheets without washing machines was hard work because
choices: the process of doing laundry involved several different stages.
the soap used to wash laundry had to be prepared by hand.
people had to travel long distances in order to dry their laundry.
people who did it had to wash many items to earn enough money.
choiceAnalysis: 洗濯の工程に複数の段階があった→正解。💡 carrying→wetting→rubbing→putting in water→removing water=複数段階
石けんは手作り→本文にない
乾燥のために長距離移動→本文にない
お金を稼ぐために多くの品を洗う→本文にない

questions.question: What was one challenge faced by people trying to invent electric washing machines?
choices: Many people thought that they would not be as effective as washing laundry by hand.
The use of electricity was limited to a few homes in wealthier parts of the world.
They could not discover how to make a machine to do all the steps involved in washing.
Machines that involve both electricity and water can be very unsafe to work with.
choiceAnalysis: 手洗いほど効果的でないと考えた→本文にない
電気が限られた家庭のみ→本文にない
全工程を行う機械を発見できなかった→実際にはmost of the stepsを自動化
電気と水を使う機械は非常に危険→正解。💡 combination of water and electricity is very dangerous=水+電気=危険

questions.question: Washing machines have allowed people to
choices: spend more time teaching themselves and their children.
use their energy for volunteer activities in their communities.
invent other machines to carry out tasks in the home.
live in parts of the world where there are many rivers and lakes.
choiceAnalysis: 自分や子供の教育に多くの時間を費やす→正解。💡 extra time and energy to study and teach their children
ボランティア活動→本文にない
他の家庭用機械を発明→本文にない
川や湖が多い地域に住む→本文にない

passages.label: C
passages.title: Living the Dream
paragraphs: On average, people spend about one-third of their lives sleeping, and for about one-quarter of the time that they are asleep, they dream. Although scientists have learned a lot about the parts of people's brains that are involved in dreaming, they are still uncertain about the purpose of dreams. One reason for this is the variety of dreams that people have — they can be pleasant, scary, unusual, or very ordinary. On top of this, they often do not make sense and are mostly forgotten soon after waking up.

People have been trying to explain why we dream for thousands of years. Ancient people believed that dreams were messages from gods. More recently, it was suggested that dreams could tell us about hidden parts of our personalities. These days, most psychologists believe that one of the principal functions of dreaming is to review memories and strengthen them. This is important because to learn well, we must not only find new ideas and skills but also regularly recall them.

For a recent study, Erin Wamsley of Furman University in the United States invited 48 participants to spend the night at a special laboratory at the university. The participants were woken up several times during the night and asked to report what they had been dreaming about. The following morning, the participants tried to connect the content of their dreams with events in their lives. Wamsley found that over half the dreams could be connected to memories of experiences. This supports the idea that dreams play a role in learning.

However, Wamsley also found that about 25 percent of dreams were connected to specific future events in participants' lives, such as upcoming tests or trips. This, she believes, is evidence that another important function of dreaming is to give people a chance to prepare for these events. Moreover, Wamsley observed that these dreams were more common later in the night. One explanation that she offers is that our brains are aware of time even while we are sleeping. As we get closer to the start of a new day, our attention switches from reviewing past events to thinking about future ones.
translations: 平均して、人は人生の約3分の1を睡眠に費やし、眠っている時間の約4分の1に夢を見る。科学者たちは夢に関わる脳の部位について多くを学んだが、夢の目的についてはまだ確信が持てていない。その理由の一つは夢の多様性だ。夢は楽しいもの、怖いもの、珍しいもの、ごく普通のものがある。さらに夢はしばしば意味をなさず、目覚めた後すぐに忘れられてしまう。
人々は何千年もの間、なぜ夢を見るのか説明しようとしてきた。古代の人々は夢は神々からのメッセージだと信じていた。より最近では、夢は私たちの性格の隠れた部分について教えてくれると示唆された。現在、ほとんどの心理学者は夢の主な機能の一つは記憶を振り返り強化することだと考えている。よく学ぶためには、新しいアイデアやスキルを見つけるだけでなく、定期的にそれらを思い出す必要があるからだ。
最近の研究で、アメリカのファーマン大学のエリン・ワムズリーは48人の参加者を大学の特別な研究室で一晩過ごすよう招いた。参加者は夜中に何度か起こされ、何の夢を見ていたか報告するよう求められた。翌朝、参加者は夢のと自分の人生の出来事を結びつけようとした。ワムズリーは半分以上の夢が経験の記憶と結びつけられることを発見した。これは夢が学習に役割を果たすという考えを裏付ける。

しかし、ワムズリーは夢の約25%が今後のテストや旅行など、参加者の将来の出来事と結びついていることも発見した。これは夢のもう一つの重要な機能はこれらの出来事に備える機会を与えることだと彼女は考えている。さらに、これらの夢は夜遅くになるほど多いことが観察された。彼女が提唱する一つの説明は、睡眠中でも脳は時間を認識しているということだ。新しい一日の始まりに近づくにつれ、注意は過去の出来事の振り返りから将来の出来事の考察に切り替わる。
sentencePairs: On average, people spend about one-third of their lives sleeping, and for about one-quarter of the time that they are asleep, they dream.
平均して、人は人生の約3分の1を睡眠に費やし、眠っている時間の約4分の1に夢を見る。
They are still uncertain about the purpose of dreams.
夢の目的についてはまだ確信が持てていない。
One reason for this is the variety of dreams that people have.
その理由の一つは夢の多様性だ。
They often do not make sense and are mostly forgotten soon after waking up.
夢はしばしば意味をなさず、目覚めた後すぐに忘れられてしまう。
Ancient people believed that dreams were messages from gods.
古代の人々は夢は神々からのメッセージだと信じていた。
Most psychologists believe that one of the principal functions of dreaming is to review memories and strengthen them.
ほとんどの心理学者は夢の主な機能の一つは記憶を振り返り強化することだと考えている。
The participants were woken up several times during the night and asked to report what they had been dreaming about.
参加者は夜中に何度か起こされ、何の夢を見ていたか報告するよう求められた。
Wamsley found that over half the dreams could be connected to memories of experiences.
ワムズリーは半分以上の夢が経験の記憶と結びつけられることを発見した。
About 25 percent of dreams were connected to specific future events in participants' lives.
夢の約25%が参加者の将来の出来事と結びついていた。
Our brains are aware of time even while we are sleeping.
睡眠中でも脳は時間を認識している。
As we get closer to the start of a new day, our attention switches from reviewing past events to thinking about future ones.
新しい一日の始まりに近づくにつれ、注意は過去の出来事の振り返りから将来の出来事の考察に切り替わる。

questions.question: What is one reason that scientists are uncertain about why people dream?
choices: Several parts of people's brains are involved in dreaming.
Dreams do not usually appear to have a clear meaning.
People often do not want to describe their dreams honestly.
Different people sometimes have exactly the same dream.
choiceAnalysis: 脳の複数の部位が関与→不確実さの理由ではない
夢に明確な意味がないように見える→正解。💡 do not make sense=意味をなさない→clear meaningがない
正直に描写したがらない→本文にない
同じ夢を見る→本文にない

questions.question: Modern psychologists think that
choices: people discovered the reason that we have dreams thousands of years ago.
people's brains are able to exercise and grow larger by having dreams.
dreams allow people to hide parts of their personalities that they do not like.
dreams give people an opportunity to make their memories stronger.
choiceAnalysis: 何千年前に理由を発見→古代の人の考えで現代心理学者ではない
脳が大きくなる→本文にない
性格の一部を隠す→「隠れた部分について教える」で違う
記憶を強くする機会→正解。💡 review memories and strengthen them=記憶を振り返り強化

questions.question: What was one thing that participants in Erin Wamsley's study were asked to do?
choices: Discuss the content of their dreams with other participants in the study.
Relate what happened in their dreams to what was happening in their lives.
Try to wake themselves up as soon as they knew that they were dreaming.
Compare their own dreams with a list of dreams that people commonly have.
choiceAnalysis: 他の参加者と夢の内容を話し合う→本文にない
夢の出来事と人生の出来事を結びつける→正解。💡 connect the content of dreams with events in their lives
夢を見ているとわかったら起きる→本文にない
よくある夢のリストと比較→本文にない

questions.question: Wamsley suggests that dreams about future events
choices: happen because our brains know that we will wake up soon.
occur more often after tests or other stressful events.
are experienced just as often as dreams about the past.
probably stay in our memories longer than other dreams.
choiceAnalysis: 脳がもうすぐ起きることを知っているから起こる→正解。💡 brains are aware of time + closer to start of new day→もうすぐ起きる=将来の夢が増える
テストなどの後に多い→テストは未来のイベントとして言及
過去の夢と同じ頻度→past 50%+ vs future 25%で違う
他の夢より記憶に残る→本文にない

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true?
choices: People long ago believed that gods spoke to them through dreams.
People dream for more than half of the time that they are asleep.
Participants in Wamsley's study mostly had dreams about future events.
Participants in Wamsley's study were observed in their own homes.
choiceAnalysis: 古代の人は神が夢で語りかけると信じた→正解。💡 dreams were messages from gods=神からのメッセージ
眠っている時間の半分以上→one-quarter(4分の1)
主に将来の夢→25%のみ。半分以上は過去の記憶
自宅で観察→大学の特別な研究室

focusPoints.title: provided (that) / as long as 条件の接続詞
focusPoints.subtitle: Conditional Conjunctions
focusPoints.explanation: provided (that)は「～という条件で」の意味でif/as long asと同義。動詞のprovideとは異なる接続詞用法。2級文法で頻出。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Yes, provided you finish your homework first.

examples.en: You can go, provided you finish your homework first.
examples.ja: 宿題を終わらせれば行ってもいいよ。

examples.en: I'll help you, as long as you ask politely.
examples.ja: 丁寧に頼めば助けてあげるよ。

examples.en: The event will take place, provided that the weather is good.
examples.ja: 天気が良ければイベントは開催される。

practiceQuestions.q: 'provided that' を 'as long as' に置き換えられるか？
practiceQuestions.a: はい。You can borrow books as long as you have a valid membership card. どちらも「～する限り」。

practiceQuestions.q: 'On condition that' は 'provided that' と同義か？
practiceQuestions.a: はい。どちらも条件を表す。On condition that = provided that = as long as。

practiceQuestions.q: このパッセージの条件表現を全て挙げると？
practiceQuestions.a: provided that, As long as, if, On condition that の4つ。

highlightPatterns: because they often could not produce

practicePassage.en: You can borrow books from the library provided that you have a valid membership card. As long as you return them within two weeks, there will be no late fees. However, if your card has expired, you must renew it before borrowing again. On condition that you follow these rules, you can borrow up to five books at a time.
practicePassage.ja: 有効な会員カードを持っていれば図書館から本を借りることができる。2週間以内に返却すれば延滞料はかからない。ただし、カードが切れている場合は、再び借りる前に更新しなければならない。これらの規則に従うことを条件に、一度に5冊まで借りられる。

focusPoints.title: 倍数表現 X times the size of Y
focusPoints.subtitle: Multiplier Expressions
focusPoints.explanation: 「XのY倍」を表す構文。X times the size/length/weight of Y の形。as...asを使う形も：X times as large as Y。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Her kitchen is four times the size of other people's kitchens.

examples.en: Her kitchen is four times the size of other people's kitchens.
examples.ja: 彼女のキッチンは他の人のキッチンの4倍の大きさだ。
examples.note: X times the size of Y

examples.en: This room is twice as large as mine.
examples.ja: この部屋は私の部屋の2倍の広さだ。
examples.note: X times as...as パターン

examples.en: The population is three times what it was 50 years ago.
examples.note: X times what S V パターン

practiceQuestions.q: 'twice the size of' を 'twice as large as' に書き換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: Russia is approximately twice as large as Canada.

practiceQuestions.q: 'four times as many people as' はどういう構造？
practiceQuestions.a: 倍数 + as + 形容詞/副詞 + as ～。「～の4倍の人数」。many は people（可算名詞）に対応。

practiceQuestions.q: 'half the area of' は何倍を意味するか？
倍数表現の基本パターンを2つ挙げると？
practiceQuestions.a: ① X times as ... as Y ② X times the + 名詞 + of Y

highlightPatterns: compared with that of washing machines

practicePassage.en: Russia is approximately twice the size of Canada, making it the largest country in the world. Tokyo's population is about three times as large as that of Berlin. The new stadium can hold four times as many people as the old one. Despite being only half the area of Texas, the United Kingdom has a population nearly twice as large.
practicePassage.ja: ロシアはカナダの約2倍の大きさで、世界最大の国だ。東京の人口はベルリンの約3倍だ。新しいスタジアムは旧スタジアムの4倍の人を収容できる。テキサスの半分の面積しかないにもかかわらず、イギリスの人口はほぼ2倍だ。

focusPoints.title: as good as any / 比較のany
focusPoints.subtitle: Comparative Any
focusPoints.explanation: as good as any は「どんなものにも劣らない」=最上級に匹敵。any は比較の文脈で「どの～でも」の強い意味になる。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Her soups and stews are as good as any that are served in a restaurant.

examples.en: Her soups are as good as any that are served in a restaurant.
examples.ja: 彼女のスープはレストランで出されるどれにも劣らない。
examples.note: as good as any=最上級に匹敵

examples.en: He is as talented as any student in this school.
examples.ja: 彼はこの学校のどの生徒にも劣らないほど才能がある。

examples.en: This is as important as any issue we have discussed.
examples.ja: これは私たちが議論したどの問題にも劣らず重要だ。

practiceQuestions.q: 'as good as any' は何を意味するか？
practiceQuestions.a: 「どれにも劣らないほど良い」。最上級に近い意味を持つ比較表現。

practiceQuestions.q: 'more skilled than any other cook' はなぜ other が必要？
practiceQuestions.a: 自分自身との比較を避けるため。比較級 + than any other + 単数名詞 = 最上級と同義。

practiceQuestions.q: 'the finest cuisine of any restaurant' のany はどんな意味？
practiceQuestions.a: 「どのレストランの中でも」。最上級 + of any = 全体の中で最高。

highlightPatterns: as good as any that are served
easier to buy for ordinary people
more common later in the night

practicePassage.en: This restaurant serves food as good as any in the city. The chef is more skilled than any other cook in the region. No dish on the menu is better than the grilled salmon. The desserts are as delicious as any I have ever tasted. In fact, many food critics say this place has the finest cuisine of any restaurant they have reviewed.
practicePassage.ja: このレストランは市内のどの店にも劣らない料理を提供する。シェフはこの地域の他のどの料理人よりも腕が良い。メニューのどの料理もグリルサーモンにはかなわない。デザートはこれまで食べたどのものにも劣らないほどおいしい。実際、多くの食評家はここがこれまで評価したどのレストランよりも最高の料理だと言う。

focusPoints.title: 前置詞+名詞のイディオム
focusPoints.subtitle: Preposition Idioms
focusPoints.explanation: in person(直接)、in charge of(担当して)、at ease(気楽に)など、前置詞+名詞の形で副詞・形容詞として機能するイディオム群。2級では毎回出題される頻出パターン。
focusPoints.sourceQuote: Applicants must apply in person. / Jason is in charge of checking.

examples.ja: 応募者は直接申し込まなければならない。
examples.note: in person=直接（郵送やオンラインではなく）

examples.en: He is in charge of the project.
examples.ja: 彼がプロジェクトの担当だ。

examples.en: The students felt at ease during the lesson.
examples.ja: 生徒たちはレッスン中リラックスしていた。
examples.note: at ease=気楽に・くつろいで

practiceQuestions.q: 'on schedule' と 'in time' の違いは？
practiceQuestions.a: on schedule = 予定通りに（計画に沿って）。in time = 間に合って（締め切り前に）。

practiceQuestions.q: 'at a loss' はどういう意味か？
practiceQuestions.a: 途方に暮れて、困って。be at a loss = 何をすべきかわからない。
better than expected / exceeded expectations / surpassed what was anticipated

practiceQuestions.q: このパッセージの前置詞+名詞イディオムを全て挙げると？
practiceQuestions.a: on schedule, At first, at a loss, in harmony, beyond expectation, In the end

practicePassage.en: The project was completed on schedule despite several unexpected problems. At first, the team was at a loss about how to solve the technical issues. However, they worked together in harmony and found creative solutions. The final product was beyond expectation, and the client was completely satisfied. In the end, the team members felt a great sense of accomplishment.
practicePassage.ja: いくつかの予想外の問題にもかかわらず、プロジェクトは予定通りに完了した。最初、チームは技術的な問題の解決方法について途方に暮れていた。しかし、調和して協力し、創造的な解決策を見つけた。最終製品は期待を超えるもので、クライアントは完全に満足した。最終的に、チームメンバーは大きな達成感を感じた。

focusPoints.title: 今回の重要なパラフレーズ
focusPoints.subtitle: Key Paraphrases
focusPoints.explanation: 正解選択肢では本文の表現が言い換えられている。同じ内容を異なる語彙・構文で表現するパターンを見抜く力が必要。
focusPoints.sourceQuote:
①various types of courses ← business, chat, exams, fun
②did not know which class ← not sure which lesson
③every weekday evening ← Monday through Friday
④several different stages ← carrying, wetting, rubbing...
⑤very unsafe ← very dangerous

examples.en: various types of courses = business, chat, exams, fun
examples.ja: 様々なタイプのコース = ビジネス、会話、試験、楽しみ
examples.note: 具体的列挙→まとめた表現

examples.en: every weekday evening = Monday through Friday
examples.ja: 毎週平日の夜 = 月曜から金曜
examples.note: weekday→具体的な曜日への言い換え

examples.ja: 非常に危険 = 非常に安全でない

practiceQuestions.q: 'support small businesses' を言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: assist local enterprises / help smaller companies / aid independent businesses
having difficulty / facing challenges / in financial trouble
recession / financial decline / period of economic difficulty

practiceQuestions.q: 'create more employment opportunities' を言い換えると？
practiceQuestions.a: generate more jobs / provide more work for people / increase job openings

highlightPatterns: business situations, to chat with friends, to pass exams
carrying the laundry to a river
impact has been small compared with
do not usually appear to have a clear meaning

practicePassage.en: The government announced a new policy to support small businesses. The plan aims to provide financial assistance to companies that are struggling. Many shop owners welcomed the decision, saying it would help them survive the economic downturn. Critics, however, argued that the funds should be directed toward larger industries that create more employment opportunities.
practicePassage.ja: 政府は中小企業を支援する新しい政策を発表した。この計画は苦戦している企業に財政支援を提供することを目的としている。多くの店主はこの決定を歓迎し、経済の低迷を乗り越えるのに役立つと述べた。しかし批判者は、より多くの雇用機会を生み出す大規模な産業に資金を向けるべきだと主張した。
